title: Making Actions with MCP and Stripe
tagline: Let a book agent take a real test-mode action by creating a Stripe checkout link through MCP.

story.problem: The agent can answer, remember, stay observable, and enforce guardrails, but production agents also need to take external actions safely.
story.solution: Connect Stripe remote MCP in test mode, require explicit user approval, and create a payment link for a fictional book purchase.
story.outcome: An action-capable agent that demonstrates MCP tool use without giving the model uncontrolled side effects.

prerequisites: A Stripe Sandbox or test-mode account
A Stripe test-mode secret key for seeding
A restricted Stripe key for remote MCP runtime calls

quickstart.configure: cp .env.example .env  # then fill NEBIUS_API_KEY, STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, and STRIPE_MCP_API_KEY
quickstart.run: docker compose up -d postgres && uv sync && make seed-stripe-books && make dev

endpoints.description: Run the guarded action-capable agent and stream approval/action events.
Approve or reject a pending Stripe MCP action.
Attach user feedback to a LangSmith run inherited from Cookbook #7.
Summarize what the agent knows about a user.
Prometheus metrics scrape endpoint, including approval and Stripe MCP counters.
